item.title: CONTENT WARNING POST
item.content:encoded: I've added a content warning to this SCP and to the more explicitly sexual SCP-3380. As we, as a community, move towards protecting our more vulnerable readers, I hope that this in-universe presentation of the possible triggers in an article becomes standard, as it both lets the reader prepare themselves, and adds tension and atmosphere instantly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh dear me…

Why does this weird mauve entity torture these two through some "so-called atonement" ritual?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a reader you can feel your perception of -1's sanity eroding as you progress through the interviews. Abuse really is a terrible thing, and this skip works as an adequate and respectful representation of it. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fun little logical fallacy there, yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm reminded somehow of SCP-1646.

I wonder what he'd think of the Carpet King.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
First of all, it stands out to me that we have a somewhat beyond comprehension creature torturing dudes for somewhat beyond comprehensible reasons, and then a month later you posted 3999 so I see a theme there. And then we got into the punishment/abuse/brainwashy and gah. +1

Also, here’s a reading: https://youtu.be/6xP6uacyB8g

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really fantastically done. It's very simple, kind of subtle, and just really unsettling. Definitely hits close to home, but in a good way for me at least. Strong +1.

Small note on a typo though!

SCP-2817-1 is William Simon Higly, a male human of mixed-race descent. Previously known as D-77810, SCP-2817 was previously serving a life sentence for involvement in a double homicide with his brother

Shouldn't this one be SCP-2817-1 again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to admit, when I read the title and the description, I thought this was headed for SCP-J-Wannabe territory. Then I got into the Interviews. (It's always in the interviews these days, isn't it?)

I think it's safe to say that so-called "Tough Love," and the frankly insane narratives its victims build around it, (I.E. "pa only beat me because he loved me," not because he was an alcoholic and a violent person who was so disappointed with the way his life went that he couldn't even stand being around his own family without picking someone to blame and lashing out at them, etc.) are responsible for most of the pain and violence in the world today.

Thank you for writing this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When The Carpet King is happy, I'm happy. I'm happy now, so he must be happy.

This is great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This hit 50+! I'm over the moon!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of my 1e AD&D days, where Gygax insisted on improving people's vocabulary with lots of questionable stylistic choices. One included making sure all the creatures were colored mauve, drab, taupe, ecru, ochre, etc.

But it is a great color.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll admit that when I read this, I had to go look up what color Mauve was, and I had a very "Oh…. Well, that's something." reaction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is solidly written, SPaG- and narrative-wise. However, I found the addenda to be a little heavy-handed and the theme of abuse and manipulation disquieting in a way I generally prefer not to experience, though.1

No vote, but well done regardless.

Footnotes
1. Personal reasons, not your execution of the topic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A lot of people have pointed out most of the things that make me upvote already, but I'd like to give special mention to one particular aspect:

The abusive otherworldly entity glows deep mauve.

This is mauve: █████████

This appeals to my inner absurdist on a deep, deep level.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you see this back during the contest days?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's interesting you saw critique of organized religion. I didn't have that in mind when writing it, but that's a cool way to see it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really glad to finally see this posted! Exactly as good and horrifically unnerving as it was the first time!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Incorporating abuse (or anything else with actual resonance in real life) into an SCP or any ind of fantastic fiction is a very delicate thing that can easily go wrong… But this…kind of manages to work, by looking at the lasting psychological effects of such experiences instead of trying to be shocking. It's pretty good, actually.

Bonus points for having the SCP be the ritual (always nice to see more abstract takes on what an SCP can be) and the description mostly anchored to a presumably non-anomalous person who's just been snared by some vague but vaguely menacing thing. It manages to be both more abstract and more grounded than most.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As original as it is fascinating, which is to say thoroughly. I definitely got the hint of the D-Class contest out of it. Too bad it couldn't be part of the actual contest, but nice to see it here regardless. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Chills! Gave me a damn case of chills. There's so much here, so many hooks to snag a reader! I doubt any two readers will be affected by this in remotely the same way, it could resonate on so many levels (face value, substance addiction, abusive relationship, organized religion, etc.) Masterful!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, back when I read the draft for this remembered the weird dream this based off of, I was expecting into be weirdly funny. I am very happy that this was the exact opposite of that.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This started out on the outskirts of Creepy Town and found its way to downtown Horror City.

I encourage prospective contributors to the site to read this and consider how much more affecting it is than a rage stating Thing What Kills You.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's like someone putting a knife under your fingernails and very, very, very slowly running the edge right up against the area where it's going to start hurting. Intimately disquieting and intensely uncomfortable in a way that not many things have achieved so far. +1'd.

ETA: Also, jesus, the abuse undertones. Christ on a bike.

item.title: The King Rises!
item.content:encoded:
"Guilt is a trap, a way for bad people to continue hurting others without ever making a real change in their lives."

This was originally written for the D-Class contest, and -2 is straight out of a dream I had, but real life intervened since then and it's a few months late. Special thanks to Cyantreuse, InitHello, Aiden Eldritch, Levi, and Rimple, and several others for support and feedback.
